  • Method
chemaxon.metabolism

Class Metabolizer

  • java.lang.Object
    • chemaxon.metabolism.Metabolizer

  • public class Metabolizer
extends Object
    Enumerates the metabolites of a given substrate with the reactions of the given biotransformation library and calculates their estimated probabilities of formation and accumulation.

    See the documentation for details.

    A simple example using the built in library: 

     // create Metabolizer
 Metabolizer metabolizer = new Metabolizer();
 
 // set the generation limit
 metabolizer.setGenerationLimit(4);
 
 // set the substrate Molecule object
 metabolizer.setSubstrate(mol);
 
 // execute enumeration and get the resulting metabolite list
 List<Metabolite> metabolites = metabolizer.enumerate();
 
 // get a list of unique metabolites sorted by global accumulation (major metabolite indicator)
 List<Metabolite> globals = metabolizer.calculateGlobals(metabolites);
 
 // convert the Metabolite list to Molecule list having calculated indicators as properties
 List<Molecule> result = Metabolizer.exportMetabolites(globals);
    Since:
    6.0
    Author:
    Gyorgy Pirok

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  Metabolizer.FormationComparator
      Comparator class for sorting metabolites by their formation values.

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      List<Metabolite> calculateGlobals(Collection<Metabolite> metabolites)
      Selects metabolites having unique chemical structures, and calculates global indicators considering all alternative routes to the same compound.
      List<Metabolite> calculateGlobals(Collection<Metabolite> metabolites, int sizeLimit, double accumulationLimit)
      Selects metabolites having unique chemical structures, and calculates global indicators considering all alternative routes to the same compound.
      void cancel()
      Stops the enumeration progress.
      List<Metabolite> enumerate()
      Enumerates the unenumerated metabolites of the substrate within the specified limits.
      List<Metabolite> enumerate(Metabolite metabolite, int generations)
      Enumerates the child metabolites of the given metabolite up to the given number of generations.
      List<Metabolite> enumerate(Metabolite metabolite, int generations, int population)
      Enumerates the child metabolites of the given metabolite up to the given number of generations and population.
      static List<Molecule> exportMetabolites(List<Metabolite> metabolites, int decimals)
      Converts a metabolite list to a molecule list storing metabolite fields as properties.
      static List<RxnMolecule> exportTransformations(List<Metabolite> metabolites, int decimals)
      Converts a metabolite list to an reaction list storing metabolite fields as properties.
      List<BiotransformationInfo> getBiotransformationInfo()
      Extracts basic information such as name and the first example from the current biotransformation library.
      List<BiotransformationInfo> getBiotransformationInfo(List<RxnMolecule> reactions)
      Extracts basic information such as name and the first example from the given biotransformation library.
      String getExcludeCondition()
      Gets a Chemical Terms expression that specifies which molecules should not be in the output of the enumeration.
      double getFastModeLimit()
      Gets the relative formation rate limit for fast enumeration mode.
      int getGenerationLimit()
      Gets the maximum number of generations allowed to be enumerated.
      double getLikelyLimit()
      Gets the accumulation limit of likely metabolites.
      int getPopulationLimit()
      Gets a limit for the number of enumerated metabolites.
      Metabolite getRoot()
      Gets the substrate as a metabolite.
      Molecule getSubstrate()
      Gets the substrate molecule.
      String getTerminationCondition()
      Gets a Chemical Terms expression that specifies which molecules should not be enumerated further.
      int getThreadCount()
      Gets the number of cpu cores used during the enummeration.
      double getUnlikelyLimit()
      Gets the accumulation limit of unlikely metabolites.
      void initialize()
      Prepares metabolizer for enumeration initializing the biotransformation library or loading the default library if no reactions have been specified.
      boolean isExhaustive()
      Gets the mode of the enumeration.
      List<Metabolite> load(List<Molecule> molecules)
      Builds the entire metabolic tree from a previously saved molecule list.
      static void main(String[] args)
      Provides command line access to Metabolizer functionalities.
      void recalculate()
      Recalculates the metabolic indicator values of all metabolites.
      void recalculate(Metabolite metabolite)
      Recalculates the metabolic indicator values of the given metabolite and its children.
      Metabolite reset()
      Resets the metabolism.
      void resetSettings()
      Resets enumeration options to their default values.
      List<Molecule> save(int decimals)
      Converts the entire metabolic tree to a molecule list storing metabolite fields as properties.
      void setExcludeCondition(String expression)
      Sets a Chemical Terms expression that specifies which molecules should not be in the output of the enumeration.
      void setExhaustive(boolean value)
      Sets the mode of the enumeration.
      void setFastModeLimit(double value)
      Sets the relative formation rate limit for fast enumeration mode.
      void setGenerationLimit(int value)
      Sets the maximum number of generations allowed to be enumerated.
      void setLikelyLimit(double value)
      Sets the accumulation limit of likely metabolites.
      void setPopulationLimit(int value)
      Sets a limit for the number of metabolites.
      void setReactions(List<RxnMolecule> reactions)
      Sets the biotransformation library containing reactions to transform the substrate to metabolites using a default scale value of 7.
      void setReactions(List<RxnMolecule> reactions, int scale)
      Sets the biotransformation library containing reactions to transform the substrate to metabolites.
      void setSubstrate(Molecule substrate, String identifier)
      Sets the substrate molecule to be metabolized.
      void setTerminationCondition(String expression)
      Sets a termination condition defined as a chemical terms expression.
      void setThreadCount(int value)
      Sets the number of cpu cores to utilise.
      void setUnlikelyLimit(double value)
      Sets the global accumulation limit of likely metabolites.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Metabolizer

        public Metabolizer()

    • Method Detail

      • calculateGlobals

        public List<Metabolite> calculateGlobals(Collection<Metabolite> metabolites)
                                  throws IOException,
                                         MetabolizerException
        Selects metabolites having unique chemical structures, and calculates global indicators considering all alternative routes to the same compound.
        Parameters:
        metabolites - a collection of metabolites to process
        Returns:
        list of metabolites with unique molecular structures sorted by global accumulation
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        IOException

      • calculateGlobals

        public List<Metabolite> calculateGlobals(Collection<Metabolite> metabolites,
                                int sizeLimit,
                                double accumulationLimit)
                                  throws IOException,
                                         MetabolizerException
        Selects metabolites having unique chemical structures, and calculates global indicators considering all alternative routes to the same compound.
        Parameters:
        metabolites - a collection of metabolites to process
        sizeLimit - the maximum number of global metabolites returned, zero or negative number means no limit
        accumulationLimit - the minimum global accumulation value required, metabolites with lower global accumulation are ignored, zero or negative number means no limit
        Returns:
        list of metabolites with unique molecular structures sorted by global accumulation
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        IOException

      • cancel

        public void cancel()
        Stops the enumeration progress.

      • enumerate

        public List<Metabolite> enumerate()
                           throws ReactionException,
                                  MetabolizerException,
                                  IOException,
                                  chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException,
                                  InterruptedException,
                                  ExecutionException
        Enumerates the unenumerated metabolites of the substrate within the specified limits. The first metabolite is the original substrate.
        Returns:
        the list of metabolites
        Throws:
        ReactionException
        MetabolizerException
        IOException
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        ExecutionException
        InterruptedException
        See Also:
        enumerate(Metabolite, int), enumerate(Metabolite, int, int)

      • enumerate

        public List<Metabolite> enumerate(Metabolite metabolite,
                         int generations)
                           throws MolFormatException,
                                  IOException,
                                  chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException,
                                  MetabolizerException,
                                  ReactionException,
                                  InterruptedException,
                                  ExecutionException
        Enumerates the child metabolites of the given metabolite up to the given number of generations. GenerationLimit and PopulationLimit constraints are ignored. Call this function to enumerate the metabolic tree interactively.
        Parameters:
        metabolite - a metabolite to process
        generations - the number of generations to return
        Returns:
        the list of child metabolites of the given compound.
        Throws:
        MolFormatException
        IOException
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        ExecutionException
        InterruptedException
        ReactionException
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        enumerate(), enumerate(Metabolite, int, int)

      • enumerate

        public List<Metabolite> enumerate(Metabolite metabolite,
                         int generations,
                         int population)
                           throws MolFormatException,
                                  IOException,
                                  chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException,
                                  MetabolizerException,
                                  ReactionException,
                                  InterruptedException,
                                  ExecutionException
        Enumerates the child metabolites of the given metabolite up to the given number of generations and population. GenerationLimit and PopulationLimit constraints are ignored. Call this function to enumerate the metabolic tree interactively.
        Parameters:
        metabolite - a metabolite to process
        generations - the number of generations to return
        Returns:
        the list of child metabolites of the given compound.
        Throws:
        MolFormatException
        IOException
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        ExecutionException
        InterruptedException
        ReactionException
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        enumerate(), enumerate(Metabolite, int)

      • getBiotransformationInfo

        public final List<BiotransformationInfo> getBiotransformationInfo()
                                                           throws MolFormatException
        Extracts basic information such as name and the first example from the current biotransformation library.
        Returns:
        a list of information records
        Throws:
        MolFormatException

      • getBiotransformationInfo

        public final List<BiotransformationInfo> getBiotransformationInfo(List<RxnMolecule> reactions)
                                                           throws MolFormatException
        Extracts basic information such as name and the first example from the given biotransformation library. Call it with null to retrieve information of the default library.
        Returns:
        a list of information records
        Throws:
        MolFormatException

      • getExcludeCondition

        public String getExcludeCondition()
        Gets a Chemical Terms expression that specifies which molecules should not be in the output of the enumeration. Excluded compounds can be common small, but insignificant fragments, they do not appear in the output, but they are considered in the indicator calculations.
        Returns:
        the exclude condition
        See Also:
        getTerminationCondition()

      • getFastModeLimit

        public double getFastModeLimit()
        Gets the relative formation rate limit for fast enumeration mode. Direct metabolites of a compound formed slower than this relative limit are ignored as they are considered to be produced in insignificant amounts. Value of 0.1 means 10% of the highest rate.
        Returns:
        the fast mode limit value
        See Also:
        isExhaustive()

      • getLikelyLimit

        public double getLikelyLimit()
        Gets the accumulation limit of likely metabolites. Metabolites having at least as high global accumulation values as this limit are considered likely ones, the rest belong to probable or unlikely categories.
        Returns:
        the accumulation limit of likely metabolites
        See Also:
        setLikelyLimit(double), getUnlikelyLimit()

      • getGenerationLimit

        public int getGenerationLimit()
        Gets the maximum number of generations allowed to be enumerated.
        Returns:
        the generation limit
        See Also:
        getPopulationLimit()

      • getPopulationLimit

        public int getPopulationLimit()
        Gets a limit for the number of enumerated metabolites. If the number of metabolites reaches this number, no further child generations will be enumerated.
        Returns:
        the maximum number of metabolites
        See Also:
        getGenerationLimit()

      • getRoot

        public Metabolite getRoot()
        Gets the substrate as a metabolite.
        Returns:
        the root of the metabolic tree

      • getSubstrate

        public Molecule getSubstrate()
        Gets the substrate molecule.
        Returns:
        the substrate

      • getTerminationCondition

        public String getTerminationCondition()
        Gets a Chemical Terms expression that specifies which molecules should not be enumerated further. Terminated metabolites are included in the output, but their children are not (they all have extinct state).
        Returns:
        the termination condition
        See Also:
        getExcludeCondition()

      • getThreadCount

        public int getThreadCount()
        Gets the number of cpu cores used during the enummeration.
        Returns:
        the number of cpu cores used for enumeration
        See Also:
        setThreadCount(int)

      • getUnlikelyLimit

        public double getUnlikelyLimit()
        Gets the accumulation limit of unlikely metabolites. Metabolites having lower global accumulation values than this limit are considered unlikely ones, the rest belong to probable or likely categories.
        Returns:
        the accumulation limit of likely metabolites
        See Also:
        setUnlikelyLimit(double), getLikelyLimit()

      • initialize

        public void initialize()
                throws MolFormatException,
                       IOException,
                       chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException,
                       MetabolizerException,
                       ReactionException
        Prepares metabolizer for enumeration initializing the biotransformation library or loading the default library if no reactions have been specified. This function is called automatically before the first enumeration if no library has been loaded yet, but it can be cal)led directly as well to pre-load the library on purpose.
        Throws:
        IOException
        MolFormatException
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        ReactionException
        MetabolizerException

      • isExhaustive

        public boolean isExhaustive()
        Gets the mode of the enumeration. When true, all metabolites are enumerated exhaustively. False value means fast mode, not enumerating metabolites of relatively slow reactions.
        Returns:
        true when the enumeration is in exhaustive mode, false when the enumeration is in fast mode
        See Also:
        getFastModeLimit()

      • load

        public List<Metabolite> load(List<Molecule> molecules)
                      throws MetabolizerException,
                             IOException
        Builds the entire metabolic tree from a previously saved molecule list.
        Returns:
        a list of molecules describing the metabolic tree
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        IOException
        See Also:
        save(int)

      • recalculate

        public void recalculate()
        Recalculates the metabolic indicator values of all metabolites. Call this function after enabling or disabling a metabolite.
        See Also:
        calculateGlobals(Collection), calculateGlobals(Collection, int, double), recalculate(Metabolite)

      • recalculate

        public void recalculate(Metabolite metabolite)
        Recalculates the metabolic indicator values of the given metabolite and its children. Call this function after enabling or disabling a metabolite. Call it with the substrate to recalculate the indicators of all metabolites.
        Parameters:
        metabolite - the metabolite to start recalculation from and downwards
        See Also:
        calculateGlobals(Collection), calculateGlobals(Collection, int, double), recalculate()

      • reset

        public Metabolite reset()
                 throws MetabolizerException
        Resets the metabolism.
        Returns:
        the root of the metabolic tree
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        resetSettings()

      • resetSettings

        public void resetSettings()
                   throws chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        Resets enumeration options to their default values. This includes generation and population limits, exclude and termination conditions, final phase, and exhaustive mode settings.
        Throws:
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        See Also:
        reset()

      • save

        public List<Molecule> save(int decimals)
        Converts the entire metabolic tree to a molecule list storing metabolite fields as properties. A saved metabolic tree can be reloaded for further enumerations.
        Parameters:
        decimals - indicators are stored as percentage with the given number of decimal digits, no rounding when negative
        Returns:
        a list of molecules describing the metabolic tree
        See Also:
        load(List)

      • setExcludeCondition

        public void setExcludeCondition(String expression)
                         throws chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        Sets a Chemical Terms expression that specifies which molecules should not be in the output of the enumeration. Excluded compounds can be common small, but insignificant fragments, they do not appear in the output, but they are considered in the indicator calculations.
        Parameters:
        expression - a Chemical Terms expression
        Throws:
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        See Also:
        setTerminationCondition(String)

      • setExhaustive

        public void setExhaustive(boolean value)
        Sets the mode of the enumeration. When true, all metabolites are enumerated exhaustively. False value means fast mode, not enumerating metabolites of relatively slow reactions.
        Parameters:
        value - true for exhaustive, false for quick enumeration mode
        See Also:
        setFastModeLimit(double)

      • setFastModeLimit

        public void setFastModeLimit(double value)
        Sets the relative formation rate limit for fast enumeration mode. Direct metabolites of a compound formed slower than this relative limit are ignored as they are considered to be produced in insignificant amounts. The default 0.1 0.1 means 10% of the highest rate.
        Parameters:
        value - the new fast mode limit
        See Also:
        setExhaustive(boolean)

      • setLikelyLimit

        public void setLikelyLimit(double value)
        Sets the accumulation limit of likely metabolites. Metabolites having at least as high global accumulation values as this limit are considered likely ones, the rest belong to probable or unlikely categories. The default value is 0.1.
        Parameters:
        value - the new global accumulation limit for likely metabolites (0..1)
        See Also:
        getLikelyLimit(), setUnlikelyLimit(double)

      • setGenerationLimit

        public void setGenerationLimit(int value)
        Sets the maximum number of generations allowed to be enumerated.
        Parameters:
        value - the new population limit
        See Also:
        setPopulationLimit(int)

      • setPopulationLimit

        public void setPopulationLimit(int value)
        Sets a limit for the number of metabolites. If the number of metabolites reaches this number, no further generations will be enumerated. The default value is zero meaning no limit.
        Parameters:
        value - the maximum number of metabolites in a generation
        See Also:
        setGenerationLimit(int)

      • setReactions

        public void setReactions(List<RxnMolecule> reactions)
                  throws MetabolizerException
        Sets the biotransformation library containing reactions to transform the substrate to metabolites using a default scale value of 7.
        Parameters:
        reactions - a list of reactions
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        setSubstrate(Molecule, String), setReactions(List, int)

      • setReactions

        public void setReactions(List<RxnMolecule> reactions,
                int scale)
                  throws MetabolizerException
        Sets the biotransformation library containing reactions to transform the substrate to metabolites.
        Parameters:
        reactions - a list of reactions
        scale - an exponent parameter for rank to rate conversion, higher scale value results larger rate difference, the default scale value is 7.
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        setSubstrate(Molecule, String)

      • setSubstrate

        public void setSubstrate(Molecule substrate,
                String identifier)
                  throws MetabolizerException
        Sets the substrate molecule to be metabolized.
        Parameters:
        substrate - the substrate molecule to metabolize
        identifier - the unique substrate identifier
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        setReactions(List, int)

      • setTerminationCondition

        public void setTerminationCondition(String expression)
                             throws chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        Sets a termination condition defined as a chemical terms expression. Metabolites fulfilling this condition are not enumerated further. Terminated metabolites are included in the output, but their children are not (they all have extinct state).
        Parameters:
        expression - a Chemical Terms expression
        Throws:
        chemaxon.nfunk.jep.ParseException
        See Also:
        setExcludeCondition(String)

      • setThreadCount

        public void setThreadCount(int value)
        Sets the number of cpu cores to utilise. Set to zero to use all available resources, this is the default setting.
        Parameters:
        value - the number of cpu cores to use, 1 for single core, 0 for all available cores
        See Also:
        getThreadCount()

      • setUnlikelyLimit

        public void setUnlikelyLimit(double value)
        Sets the global accumulation limit of likely metabolites. Metabolites having lower global accumulation values than this limit are considered unlikely ones, the rest belong to probable or likely categories. The default value is 0.001.
        Parameters:
        value - the new global accumulation limit for unlikely metabolites (0..1)
        See Also:
        getUnlikelyLimit(), setLikelyLimit(double)

      • exportMetabolites

        public static List<Molecule> exportMetabolites(List<Metabolite> metabolites,
                               int decimals)
                                        throws MolFormatException
        Converts a metabolite list to a molecule list storing metabolite fields as properties.
        Parameters:
        metabolites - a list of metabolites
        decimals - round the floating point values to the given number of decimals.
        Returns:
        a list of exported molecules
        Throws:
        MolFormatException
        IOException
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        exportTransformations(List, int)

      • exportTransformations

        public static List<RxnMolecule> exportTransformations(List<Metabolite> metabolites,
                                      int decimals)
                                               throws MolFormatException
        Converts a metabolite list to an reaction list storing metabolite fields as properties.
        Parameters:
        metabolites - a list of metabolites
        decimals - round the floating point values to the given number of decimals.
        Returns:
        a list of exported molecules
        Throws:
        MolFormatException
        IOException
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        exportMetabolites(List, int)

      • main

        public static void main(String[] args)
                 throws Exception
        Provides command line access to Metabolizer functionalities.
        Parameters:
        args - command line arguments, execute with -h argument for help
        Throws:
        Exception
